title: Server certificates should be verified during SSL/TLS connections
description: Transport Layer Security (TLS) provides secure communication between systems over the internet by encrypting the data sent between them. Certificate validation adds an extra layer of trust and security to this process to ensure that a system is indeed the one it claims to be. When certificate validation is disabled, the client skips a critical security check.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
// Disabling certificate validation globally
ServicePointManager.ServerCertificateValidationCallback = (sender, certificate, chain, sslPolicyErrors) => true;

logic.detection_conditions: Certificate validation callback that always returns true

conditions: Delegate that always returns true for certificate validation

exclusion_conditions: Callbacks that verify certificate properties (Subject, Thumbprint)
Callbacks with conditional return based on errors parameter
never_trigger_on: Callbacks checking certificate Subject or Thumbprint
logic.reasoning: Disabled certificate validation exposes connections to man-in-the-middle attacks. Callbacks that unconditionally return true skip critical security checks. Uses custom function to detect always-true patterns while excluding legitimate validation with error/certificate checks.

should_not_trigger: callback = (m, c, ch, e) => c.GetCertHashString() == expectedThumbprint;
